name: Screenshot Comparison Testing
description: Implement visual regression testing with Playwright to detect unintended UI changes by comparing screenshots against golden baselines.

steps.title: Write a visual comparison test
steps.description: Create a test that captures and compares a full-page screenshot.

steps.title: Generate baseline screenshots
steps.description: Run the test for the first time to create golden reference images.
steps.tip: Baseline screenshots are stored in a __snapshots__ directory next to your test file. Commit these to version control.

steps.title: Run the visual comparison
steps.description: Execute the tests to compare current screenshots against baselines.
steps.warning: Visual tests are sensitive to rendering differences across OS and browser versions. Always generate baselines in the same environment where tests run (typically CI).

steps.title: Configure comparison thresholds
steps.description: Adjust sensitivity settings in the Playwright config.
steps.tip: Set 'animations: disabled' to prevent flaky tests caused by mid-animation screenshots.

steps.title: Review visual diffs in the report
steps.description: Open the HTML report to see side-by-side comparisons of failures.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Playwright Visual Regression Testing
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Visual Regression Test Workflow ==="

# Check for existing snapshots
SNAPSHOT_DIR="tests/__snapshots__"
if [ -d "$SNAPSHOT_DIR" ]; then
  SNAPSHOT_COUNT=$(find "$SNAPSHOT_DIR" -name '*.png' | wc -l)
  echo "Existing baselines: $SNAPSHOT_COUNT screenshots"
else
  echo "No baselines found. Will generate on first run."
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Running Visual Tests ==="
if [ "${1:-}" = "--update" ]; then
  echo "Updating baseline screenshots..."
  npx playwright test tests/visual.spec.ts --update-snapshots
  echo ""
  echo "New baselines generated. Review and commit them:"
  find "$SNAPSHOT_DIR" -name '*.png' -newer package.json 2>/dev/null | head -10
else
  npx playwright test tests/visual.spec.ts || true
  echo ""
  echo "=== View Report ==="
  echo "Run: npx playwright show-report"
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Commands ==="
echo "Run tests:       npx playwright test tests/visual.spec.ts"
echo "Update baselines: npx playwright test tests/visual.spec.ts --update-snapshots"
echo "View report:     npx playwright show-report"

faq.question: Why do visual tests fail across different operating systems?
faq.answer: Font rendering, anti-aliasing, and sub-pixel rendering differ between macOS, Linux, and Windows. Always generate baseline screenshots in the same environment where tests run. Use Docker or CI as the canonical environment for consistency.

faq.question: How do I handle dynamic content in visual tests?
faq.answer: Mask dynamic elements with 'await expect(page).toHaveScreenshot({ mask: [page.locator('.timestamp')] })'. You can also use page.evaluate to freeze dates, hide ads, or replace dynamic content before taking the screenshot.

faq.question: What is maxDiffPixelRatio vs threshold?
faq.answer: maxDiffPixelRatio sets the maximum acceptable percentage of different pixels (0.01 = 1%). threshold sets the color difference sensitivity per pixel (0 = exact match, 1 = any color). Use both together for optimal results.
